title: [FIRRTL] Missed optimization opportunity with array with identical elements
body:
When all elements of the array are the same, we should be able to optimize away the array-indexing operation.

```firrtl
circuit Foo :
  module Foo :
    input in0 : UInt<1>
    input in1 : UInt<1>
    input address : UInt<2>
    
    output out : UInt<1>

    node idStall = or(in0, in1) 

    wire sourceStall : UInt<1>[4]
    sourceStall[0] <= idStall
    sourceStall[1] <= idStall
    sourceStall[2] <= idStall
    sourceStall[3] <= idStall
    out <= sourceStall[address]
```
Produces:

```verilog
// Generated by CIRCT unknown git version
module Foo(
  input        in0,
               in1,
  input  [1:0] address,
  output       out);

  wire       idStall = in0 | in1;
  wire [3:0] _GEN = {{idStall}, {idStall}, {idStall}, {idStall}};
  wire       _GEN_0;
  assign _GEN_0 = _GEN[address] /* cadence map_to_mux */; /* synopsys infer_mux_override */
  assign out = _GEN_0;
endmodule
```

I would expect the output to be:
```verilog
// Generated by CIRCT unknown git version
module Foo(
  input        in0,
               in1,
  input  [1:0] address,
  output       out);

  wire       idStall = in0 | in1;
  assign out = idStall;
endmodule
```
